refactor_input_function.input_function: Input function. Used to determine surrounding function and its properties.

refactor_output_function.output_function: Output function. Reference point where extracted function can be inserted before of.
refactor_output_function.output_function.comment: Comment. Optional comment above and attached to `output_function`.

refactor_scope.scope: Scope. Should contain all identifiers available in the scope (including parameters).
refactor_scope.scope.inside: Inside of a scope. Optional list of nodes spanning the whole "body" of the scope.

refactor_function.function.arg: Function arguments. Should match all arguments of a function
refactor_function.function.comment: Comment. Optional comment above and attached to `function`.
refactor_function.function.outside: Outside of a function. Optional reference point where text can be inserted outside of a function.
refactor_function.return.value: Return statement values. Should match all values returned by the return statement.

refactor_function_call.function_call.name: Function call identifier.
refactor_function_call.function_call.arg: Function call parameters. Should match all parameters of a function call.
refactor_function_call.function_call.outside: Outside of a function call. Optional reference point where text can be inserted ourisde of a function_call.

refactor_variable.variable.identifier: Variable identifier. Should match all identifier in a declaration (i.e. more than one in multiple assignments)
refactor_variable.variable.identifier_separator: Variable identifier separator. Optional separator between identifiers on multiple assignments.
refactor_variable.variable.value: Variable value. Should match all values in a declaration (i.e. more than one in multiple assignments)
refactor_variable.variable.value_separator: Variable value separator. Optional separator between values on multiple assignments.

refactor_output_statement.output_statement: Output statement. Reference point where output statement can be inserted before/after of. Should contain the range for which it is valid.
refactor_output_statement.output_statement.inside: Inside of an output statement. Optional reference point to override the reference point of `output_statement`

refactor_debug_path.debug_path_segment: Debug path segment. Should contain the range for which it's valid

eq.description: checks for equality between two nodes, or a node and a string

any-of.description: match any of the given strings against the text corresponding to a node

contains.description: match a string against parts of the text corresponding to a node

match.description: Match a regexp against the text corresponding to a node

lua-match.description: match a Lua pattern against the text corresponding to a node

has-ancestor.description: match any of the given node types against all ancestors of a node

has-parent.description: match any of the given node types against the direct ancestor of a node

kind-eq.description: checks whether a capture corresponds to a given set of nodes

set.description: sets key/value metadata for a specific match or capture

offset.description: Takes the range of the captured node and applies an offset. This will set a new range in the form of a list like { {start_row}, {start_col}, {end_row}, {end_col} } for the captured node with `capture_id` as `metadata[capture_id].range`.

gsub.description: Transforms the content of the node using a Lua pattern. This will set a new `metadata[capture_id].text`.

trim.description: Trims whitespace from the node. Sets a new `metadata[capture_id].range`. Takes a capture ID and, optionally, four integers to customize trimming behavior (`1` meaning trim, `0` meaning don't trim). When only given a capture ID, trims blank lines (lines that contain only whitespace, or are empty) from the end of the node (for backwards compatibility). Can trim all whitespace from both sides of the node if parameters are given.

set-type.description: Adds a type to the identifier/s in a match. Sets a new `metadata.types`.Takes a language name, a capture for the type and a capture for one or more identifiers.

infer-type.description: Infers a type for the identifier/s in a match. Sets a new `metadata.types`.Takes a language name and a capture for one or more identifiers.
